title: This Is Why Your Channel Isn't Growing

transcript.text: Most creators, when they get serious about growing on YouTube, go about it the completely wrong way. First, they find the [music] biggest channel in their niche, and they'll do things like reverse engineer the thumbnails, copy the title structure one-for-one, and they make [music] what they think is like a slightly better version of the video they just watched. The opportunity cost for that viewer from switching from the established creator to you is relatively high, and they're get very little reward. In fact, it's hardly noticeable because the person they're switching to is basically just doing the same thing. In that scenario, you just become another brown cow. You don't want to be better. Better is invisible. What you want to be is different. That is when people notice. Once you see the patterns, you can build your own version for your channel.
